title: About maven-shred-utils vulnerability (CVE-2022-29599)
body:
Hello
I'm the one who recently started using skopeo 1.0.0.

-Is it affected by the maven-shred-utils vulnerability (CVE-2022-29599) while using skopeo1.0.0?


By the way, I asked here about Log4J vulnerability last year, is this the right way to ask here every time I want to know?


Best regards,

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @kousui-dev. Skopeo has no dependency on maven, so it's not impacted by the listed CVE.

Note that Skopeo v1.0.0 is already two years old.  I suggest updating to a more recent version.

> By the way, I asked here about Log4J vulnerability last year, is this the right way to ask here every time I want to know?

Feel welcome to reach out here at anytime. Note that upstream, we don't offer support for that old versions of Skopeo.  In case you are using an enterprise distribution such as RHEL, the vendor is responsible for providing backports which means you have to reach out to the individual company (e.g., Red Hat support).

> By the way, I asked here about Log4J vulnerability last year, is this the right way to ask here every time I want to know?

I’d welcome if you did _some_ analysis to see whether the vulnerability could even plausibly apply. I don’t understand why you are asking about Java vulnerabilities, about these two Java vulnerabilities in particular, and ultimately it just wouldn’t scale for us to individually answer “no” to the 29599 vulnerabilities already reported in 2022 so far.
